summary: mpactr provides filters to identify and remove mispicked ions, which are isotopic patterns that are incorrectly split during preprocessing.
The filter_mispicked_ions function checks for similar ions based on retention time and mass using the parameters ringwin, isowin, trwin and max_iso_shift.
mpactr is built on an R6 class-system, meaning it operates on reference semantics in which data is updated in-place.
Reference semantics in R6 classes differ from shallow copy, where only data pointers are copied, and from deep copy, where the entire data object is copied in memory.
The default setting for the copy_object parameter in mpactr filters is FALSE, which operates on reference semantics.
Using the default copy_object = FALSE makes for an extremely fast and memory-efficient way to chain mpactr filters together.
mpactr uses R6 environments to enable reference semantics and fast filter execution.
Functions in R rely on call-by-value semantics where parameterized values are treated as local variables.
R6 classes allow sending variables by reference to functions using environment semantics.
Memory usage is improved when using R6 classes compared to traditional copy-by-value workflows.
